texto: And she was not beautiful asleep. Her expression slack and not angelic. The very ordinariness of it so beautiful he felt a yearning to be something more than he was or could be. And as good a player as he was, he knew as he turned on the reel to reel and hugged the Fender once again that nothing he composed would ever be as beautiful as her ordinary sleep.Watching her he played the music of her sleeping. And by surrendering made something beautiful.
explicacao: Citação de Steven R. Boyett sobre temas ligados a amor. “And she was not beautiful asleep. Her expression slack and not angelic. The very ordinariness of …” articula amor, beauty, love em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como termino e love.

frase_original: And she was not beautiful asleep. Her expression slack and not angelic. The very ordinariness of it so beautiful he felt a yearning to be something more than he was or could be. And as good a player as he was, he knew as he turned on the reel to reel and hugged the Fender once again that nothing he composed would ever be as beautiful as her ordinary sleep.Watching her he played the music of her sleeping. And by surrendering made something beautiful.

texto: You ask me what it feels like to have wings. I can only tell you the feeling with words. And words have neither feelings nor wings. Words are leaky vessels into which a cargo of meaning and emotion are placed, and when they leave you and reach the farther shore of another mind a considerable portion of that cargo has been lost at sea. Fallen overboard, gone to rot, consumed by vermin, decayed to a state unlike its original form.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Steven R. Boyett: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “You ask me what it feels like to have wings. I can only tell you the feeling with words. And word…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, feelings, mortality-bridge pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mortality-bridge e wings.
